reviews.review_title: Reload a spear my a s s
reviews.review_description: What do you mean reload a spear

This is hands down the best and most unique swordplay game I’ve experienced in VR. It’s the first game where swinging a sword actually felt like swinging a real sword. The weight, timing, and physicality are done so well that every fight feels immersive and satisfying.

The game keeps things simple on the surface, but the mechanics have enough depth to prevent tactics like “tickling” someone to death with rapid, unrealistic hits. Combat feels skill-based and fair, while still being intuitive enough that it never breaks immersion. You’re not fighting the controls—you’re fighting your opponent.

My only real complaint is the graphics. They’re not the strongest by today’s standards, but considering the game’s age, that’s understandable. It would be amazing to see the developers update the textures and visuals to match the quality of its sequel. A visual refresh would easily bring this game back into the spotlight.

If you enjoy VR sword fighting, you absolutely need to try this. It’s an incredible experience—and I’d love to see more players in-game. Hope to cross blades with you soon!

When you play this game, it’s important to remember that it was designed from the ground up as a competitive PvP experience. Many of the so-called “flaws” people criticize are actually core mechanics that support fair and skill-based gameplay.

Slow motion exists to give players time to properly react to attacks and to dramatically reduce the impact of latency.

One-sided combat ensures that hits are registered based only on the client of the player being hit. If you’ve ever played Broken Edge, you probably know the frustration of landing a perfect, clean stab to your opponent’s face only to be the one who gets hit, despite clearly landing your hit first. Ironlights avoids that problem.

Weapon breaking and refreshing prevents players from cheesing fights by wildly flailing their weapon without intent or control.

At its core, Ironlights is about skill, finesse, and precision. It forces you to optimize your movements, read your opponent, and adapt to their strategy. Every action is deliberate.

Unfortunately, the game has one major downside: it has been fully abandoned by the developer. The player base is so small that finding matches, especially in mid ranks, is often impossible.

Because of that, I honestly can’t recommend buying this absolute gem despite how good it is simply due to the lack of active players.

I mean... I like it, but there are quite a few issues with it. Firstly, the game is so empty!! Its so sad that no one really plays it. Secondly, despite the great replayability, it can get boring after a while. Even if they just add one more game mode and a few more classes it would really make a difference because its the same thing. The only reason that the sequel doesn't have this problem is because it is an open world story. Not to mention that you can't share your fighter creations without a code or it being featured. You have to go on to a third party website to do it and it is a lot of hassle. Although, it isn't all bad. Like I said, it has amazing replayability and it even has a feature that allows you to create your own stuff. I love the cosmetics and the colours you can customised your avatar with. Overall, I give it a 3/5. I recommend it, however I think there are a few issues that the devs need to work on.

reviews.review_title: amazing game, just a few issues.
reviews.review_description:
1. the replayability could use some work. on the surface, the game has 10 characters and over 100 fights in the campaign alone. however, every regular opponent is a bit similar. one monk fights the same as any other monk. eventually it just becomes "how do i get past the ai's defenses (which are really good btw, as is the rest of the ai  system)." perhaps more weapon variety like the exhibition matches would be nice, along with more fighting styles for regular opponents.

2. balancing is kinda nonexistent. i main monk (one of the stronger classes), and i've found that some characters are simply worse than others. crusaders, for example, can absolutely annihilate most players in melee or ranged combat, then block everything with the strongest shield in the game. meanwhile, the samurai has one sword and a weak shield at the sword's base. it's pretty useable, but you're usually better off playing knight which is arguably better at defense and range, with the capability to deal double damage in melee. some classes could use a buff (samurai, dragoon, and barbarian) while others (crusader, ninja and its charged shots, and possibly reaper) need a bit of a nerf.

3. sandbox weapons are fun to use, but most are garbage. take, for example, the scimitar. it looks cool, you can spin it, it's pretty neat. however, it's slow as hell, so even if you're dual wielding them, they're pretty bad. others, like the long spear, are a complete joke. meanwhile, there are some insane combos, especially those involving shields. overall, sandbox weapons could all use some tweaking.

to end off this review...

the game is amazing buy it right now PLEASE i need more people to fight online

EDIT: i've played a little more, and i main samurai now. i know i sorta called it trash in section 2, but it's actually really good, even better than knight. it's incredibly fast, and because of that its dps is amazing. while it's not great at defense, i've never had the shield fully break and i only get hit if i mistime my parries. i thought knight was good because lower-level players and npcs have trouble blocking it. also, i figured out how spears and whatnot work, and have even made a few sandbox fighters of my own. i'll post codes for them soon.

To start off, this game takes on a gladiator type style of fighting. The graphics are great, and are bright and fun. This aspect of the game really brightens the mood up. Now we move on to the actual fighting. The slow motion is tough to get used to at first, but once you get used to it, you can start blocking easier. The ranged attacks are fast and hard to block, but you can move around to help you dodge them. I have been blown away by this game, and I absolutely love it! Can't wait to see future updates!

reviews.review_title: Fun if you wanna play against bots
reviews.review_description:
PLEASE READ!!!!!!!!!
The game itself is interesting and fun if you only wanna play the single player mode, One there's not enough people to find a game quickly you could be queuing from 15 seconds (if your lucky) to around 5 minutes. Those aren't the longest questions times but the playing against the people is frustrating first of all there are three god tier classes first is the scythe, then the flail and finally the hammer the reason why is they will always reach around your guard as can basically do half your health in one attack faze. Dev's have done nothing to fix it such as lowering damage or reducing range and so the players abuse it. Then theres the high tier players who either abuse the aforementioned classes or most have horrible ping and will basically hit you no matter what. That being said the single player campaign is extremely fun even on the hardest difficulty it still feels like your in a movie. I would say don't buy this game it's not entirely a waste but it gets repetitive and boring after like 5 hours of gameplay.
